content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 8, 1991


Kimberly Schuld
Legislative Aide
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

Re:  Your Request For Informal Advice
     Our File No.  I-91-322

Dear Ms. Schuld:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Assemblyman Paul
Horcher concerning the gift provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹  Because of the general nature of your inquiry, your
request is treated as one for informal assistance.²

QUESTION

     If Assemblyman Horcher pays only $750 of a $1,000 airport
parking bill, and the balance is forgiven, what is the value of
any gift to him from the airport?

CONCLUSION

     If Assemblyman Horcher pays only $750 of a $1,000 parking
bill, he receives a gift of $250 from the airport.


_________________________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2  In an earlier advice letter to you, Schuld Advice Letter, A-
91-074, we discussed Assemblyman Horcher's use of campaign funds
to pay for a parking pass.

2  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 84113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-322
Page 2

FACTS

Assemblyman Horcher has a VIP parking pass for parking at
Ontario Airport. The airport annually bills the assemblyman based
upon the number of days the pass is used. You have asked us to
assume that Assemblyman Horcher may receive a bill of $1,000 for
use of the parking pass. (We assume that the $1,000 is the fair
market value of the parking privileges received by Assemblyman
Horcher.) The airport is willing to waive all or part of the
bill. However, Assemblyman Horcher does not wish to run afoul of
any gift limitations. He is considering paying $750 of the $1,000
bill.

ANALYSIS

Under Section 82028(a), "'Gift' means . . . any payment to
the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not
received . . . ." If Assemblyman Horcher pays the airport $750
for the $1,000 bill, the airport will have received consideration
of $750. The gift would only be the amount not covered by the
consideration, or $250. Please note that under recently enacted
ethics laws, Assemblyman Horcher is limited to $250 in gifts from
any single source per calendar year. Thus if he were to receive a
gift of $250 from the airport, he would be precluded from
receiving another gift from the airport for the remainder of the
calendar year.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.³

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mark T. Morodomi
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MM:dg

³ Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
